key_themes: Record Q1 2026 earnings beat (revenue $5.41B +13% YoY, EPS $2.25 +42% YoY)
AI initiatives, operational leverage, and margin expansion (37% pretax margin)
Raised 2026 revenue guidance (~6% growth) and strong NII/fee income outlook
Widespread analyst price target increases ($136–$150) with Buy/Outperform ratings
New 52-week highs, momentum, $10B buyback authorization, and strong capital returns
summary: BNY Mellon (BK) delivered exceptional Q1 2026 results with record revenue of $5.41 billion and adjusted EPS of $2.25, significantly beating estimates on broad-based growth in fees, net interest income, assets under custody/administration, and management. The company raised its full-year revenue guidance, highlighted AI-driven transformation and efficiency gains, announced a new $10B share repurchase program, and reported strong operating leverage and margin expansion. Investor and analyst reaction has been strongly bullish, with the stock hitting new all-time highs, multiple upward price target revisions to the $136–$150 range, and positive social media momentum.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/BK/)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/BK)[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/BK/)
sources: 15+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, company release, CNBC, TipRanks), 10+ recent X posts discussing earnings beats, PT raises, and new highs
